description: Left-side lateral crossing truck on a normal road, matching the active right-drift geometry, but with a pedestrian occupying the right-side escape corridor. The grid stresses the uncovered escape-side-pedestrian boundary at short TTC while varying crossing speed and ego speed.
hypothesis: When the right escape side is occupied by a pedestrian, the usual right-drift response should give way to braking at moderate TTC/low ego speed, with only the shortest-TTC/high-speed cases favoring a non-right evasive maneuver.
